Amtech'S Demurrer To First Amended Cross-Complaint, Motion To Strike And Motion To Sever
Off Calendar May-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sara Kristin Egide, Esq. (415-617-2219) of Tucker Ellis LLP for Amtech, cross-defendant & moving party / Sophia Karkasis Perlegos, Esq. (925-930-6600) for Nick Volente, Kamran Ardebilchi & Jahangir Ardebilchi, cross-complainants & opposing parties / Mark Hossmand, Esq. (415-318-5709) for Louis Gamache, plaintiff

ARGUED - Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments, tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Amtech'S Demurrer To First Amended Cross-Complaint, Motion To Strike And Motion To Sever - Cross-Defendant Amtech's Motion to Strike First Amended Cross-Complaint. Execution is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Cross-Complainant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that Court's order on Motion for Judgment on the pleadings, which is limited in scope to the pleadings before the Court at the time of the motion, allows addition of new causes of action in the amended complaint pursuant to that order. Therefore, Motion to Strike is DENIED as to causes of action 2 (comparative indemnity) and 4 (contribution) as it can be ascertained from the sole cause of action in Cross-Complaint that Cross-complainants attempted to allege such causes of action. See 6. GRANTED as to other causes of action as they were not previously alleged in the sole cause of action and no leave to amend was granted, they also do not arise for the same facts/do not relate back as Cross-Complaint was devoid of any facts.

Cross-Defendant Amtech's Demurrer to First Amended Cross-Complaint is Moot in part and SUSTAINED with leave to amend in part. Demurrer is moot as to causes of action 3, 5, 6. Sustained with leave to amend as to 1st, 2nd, 4th. There are no factual allegations contained in the FACC. Allegations from Fourth Amended Complaint incorporated by reference and without any limitations make it impossible to ascertain for which acts Defendants seek indemnity and how most of those acts relate to Cross-Defnednat. E.g. See Fourth Amended Complaint 41, 44, 64, 66.

Cross-Defendant Amtech's Motion to Sever and Vacate Trial date as to Cross-Complaint only is granted.

Three orders are signed in open court. Judge A. James Robertson II, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS NICK VOLENTE, KAMRAN ARDEBILCHI, JAHANGIR ARDEBILCHI AND PRIME PACIFIC INVESTMENTS, INC.'S motion to contest the determination of good faith settlement is denied, and the settlement is found to be in good faith. The evidence demonstrates that Mega did no work on the project and the Court has already determined that Mega's agent (Blue Light) reasonably settled for $12,500. Plaintiff's claims of $5,000,000 in damages (or other figures such as $2,000,000 in economic damages) are unsupported. A review of the other Techbilt factors reveals no basis to find the settlement not in good faith. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PRIME PACIFIC INVESTMENTS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Mark Hooshmand of the Hooshmand Law Group (318.5709) for plaintiffs and responding parties; Christina N. Dabis and John Zanghi of Zanghi Torres Arshawsky LLP (977.0444) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action 1, 3, 5, 6, 9, 11, and 13. These claims are to be asserted against the landlords and/or owners. The parties agree and judicially noticeable documents show that defendant is not the legal owner of the premises. There is no authority supporting plaintiff's "beneficial owner" theory of liability. The Court also rejects plaintiff's alter ego theory of liability. (Postal Instant Press, Inc. v. Kaswa Corp. (2008) 162 Cal.App.4th 1510). Sustained without leave to amend as to cause of action 7 because it is not an independent claim and it is repetitive of the negligence cause of action. Overruled as to causes of action 2, 4, 8, 10, and 12. Paragraphs 52-56 of the fourth amended complaint allege that defendant had a role in managing the construction at the property and failed to perform that task in a competent fashion. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

Mark Hooshmand, Esq., Hooshmand Law Group, 415-318-5700, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT MELVIN A. MORAN DBA MEGA ELECTRIC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second, eighth, tenth, eleventh and twelfth causes of action, sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth, seventh, thirteenth and fourteenth causes of action. There is a reasonable probability that Plaintiffs can establish a community of interest and an ascertainable class. Plaintiffs are granted leave to plead what duty was owed by this Defendant and how it was breached; to plead what extreme and outrageous conduct this Defendant committed; to plead facts to support the tenth and eleventh claims; to plead how the actions of this Defendant constitute a nuisance. Constructive eviction results only from a landlord's material breach of the rental agreement. There is no relationship between this Defendant and Plaintiffs that supports the seventh claim. Civil Code 1942.4 only applies to landlords. The fourteenth claim is duplicative of the first claim. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
